mission.title: Install and Configure Strimzi Kafka Operator
mission.description: Strimzi Kafka Operator allows you to run Apache Kafka on Kubernetes or OpenShift. It simplifies the deployment and management of Kafka clusters, making it suitable for production environments. Key trade-offs include the need for Kubernetes expertise and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add the Strimzi Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Strimzi Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add strimzi https://strimzi.io/charts/

helm repo update

```

> This command adds the Strimzi repository.
> Updates the local Helm chart repository cache.

steps.title: Install the Strimzi Kafka Operator
steps.description:
This step installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator in the 'kafka' namespace using Helm.

```bash

kubectl create namespace kafka

helm install strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

```

> Creates a namespace for the Kafka operator.
> Installs the Strimzi Kafka Operator.

steps.title: Create a Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a Kafka cluster using a custom resource definition (CRD).

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator/0.44.0/examples/kafka/kafka.yaml

```

> Deploys a sample Kafka cluster.

steps.title: Verify the Kafka cluster
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the Kafka cluster to ensure it is running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

kubectl describe kafka my-cluster -n kafka

```

> Lists all Kafka clusters in the 'kafka' namespace.
> Describes the Kafka cluster to check its status.

uninstall.title: Uninstall the Strimzi Kafka Operator
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Strimzi Kafka Operator and all associated resources. Be aware that this will delete the Kafka cluster and any data stored within it.

```bash

helm uninstall strimzi --namespace kafka

kubectl delete namespace kafka

```

> Uninstalls the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Deletes the Kafka namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade the Strimzi Kafka Operator
upgrade.description:
Backup your Kafka cluster, cordon nodes if necessary, upgrade the operator, and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

helm upgrade strimzi strimzi/strimzi-kafka-operator --namespace kafka --version 0.44.0

kubectl get kafka -n kafka

```

> Backs up the current Kafka cluster configuration.
> Upgrades the Strimzi Kafka Operator.
> Verifies that the Kafka cluster is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get kafka -n kafka -o yaml > kafka-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kafka cluster is not ready'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka cluster may not have completed its initialization.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kafka pods with 'kubectl get pods -n kafka'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to connect to Kafka broker'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka broker may not be running or accessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the broker's status with 'kubectl get pods -n kafka' and check logs with 'kubectl logs <broker-pod-name> -n kafka'.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources to deploy Kafka'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kubernetes cluster may not have enough resources available.

**Fix:**
Check resource allocation and consider scaling your nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Kafka topic creation failed'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The Kafka cluster may not be fully initialized or there may be permission issues.

**Fix:**
Ensure the Kafka cluster is ready and check RBAC permissions.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for Kafka 3.4.0, improved metrics collection.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated the use of 'KafkaUser' CRD in favor of 'KafkaUser' with new fields.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: Update your KafkaUser CRDs to match the new schema.

resolution.summary: You should see the Kafka cluster listed with 'kubectl get kafka -n kafka' and its status should be 'Ready'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running with sufficient resources.
